reference.name: chores
reference.context: Canonical reference for outstanding chores for the project. Chores are small actionable items that need to be addressed, but do not take priority over active development work.

chore.synopsis: Absorb redundant-read-guard hook as a builtin plugin

chores.chore:
Source: ../claude-lib/.claude/hooks/rct/redundant-read-guard.ts. Two-part scope.
(1) Complete the TODO(human) `decide()` implementation. Inputs: turnsSinceRead, bytesSinceRead, secondsSinceRead, fileSize. Base thresholds at ~5KB file size: turns=3, bytes=5000, seconds=60. Scale thresholds inversely with fileSize so larger files trip the guard sooner and tiny helpers stay "fresh" longer. Return one of three zones: clearly fresh -> `block` with redirect text Claude reads to reuse the prior read; near-threshold -> `warn` with a short message Claude reasons over before re-reading; clearly stale -> `allow` with empty message. Cover the decision with tests against `src/plugin/types.ts` PluginHookInput shapes.
(2) Package as a workspace plugin at plugins/rct-plugin-redundant-read-guard/ matching the layout of existing builtins (e.g. plugins/rct-plugin-tasktools/). The current `default export { name, trigger }` already conforms to RCTPlugin -- preserve it. Then register in two places: BUILTIN_PLUGINS in src/constants.ts and optionalDependencies in the root package.json (both as workspace:*). Verify resolution via the built-in -> local -> package chain in src/plugin/resolve.ts and add a smoke test that loading the plugin by name yields a working trigger.
Acceptance: `bun check` passes; loading the plugin by built-in name in a fresh rct.config.json registers the PreToolUse(Read) guard end-to-end.
